serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Alla Pastora pasta is approximately 400 g, equivalent to a full plate as typically served in a casual Italian dining context in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pasta with meat sauce (SR Legacy) and component ingredient data for bacon, mushrooms, olive oil, garlic
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Alla Pastora' exists in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for pasta with bacon and mushroom sauce components (USDA FDC IDs: 168936 cooked pasta, 168319 bacon, 169251 mushrooms, 748608 olive oil). The input record values (energy 187.5 kcal, protein 7.5 g, fat 11.25 g, carbohydrate 15.0 g per 100 g) are consistent with a bacon-and-mushroom pasta dish at approximately 40% pasta, 15% bacon, 20% mushrooms, 10% olive oil by weight and are retained as the best available estimate. Secondary micronutrient values (calcium 37.5 mg, iron 0.75 mg, cholesterol 22.5 mg, vitamin C 1.25 mg) are plausible for this composition and are retained with medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Alla Pastora. GI estimated at 45 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for spaghetti / pasta (white, boiled, GI ~46, mean of multiple studies) as the closest analogous food. The presence of fat (olive oil, bacon) and protein slows gastric emptying and is expected to modestly lower the effective GI relative to plain boiled pasta. GL per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving (15.0 g/100 g × 400 g = 60 g) / 100 = 27.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 275 mg/100 g from cured bacon and processed ingredients; a 400 g serving delivers approximately 1100 mg sodium, representing 48–55% of the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced bacon and no added salt, as a full 400 g serving provides approximately 1100 mg sodium — more than half the recommended daily maximum.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 15 g/100 g with estimated GI of 45 (low); a 400 g serving delivers 60 g total carbohydrate, which is a significant glycaemic load despite the low GI.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit serving size to 150–200 g to keep carbohydrate intake below 30 g per meal, and should request whole-wheat pasta to further reduce the glycaemic impact; the fat and protein content moderately slows glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.5 g/100 g from bacon and cream; a 400 g serving delivers 18 g saturated fat, approaching or exceeding the daily limit of 20 g for cardiovascular risk management.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit this dish to a 150–200 g portion and request reduced bacon and a lighter sauce, as a full serving contributes approximately 18 g saturated fat and 1100 mg sodium — both significant cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Bacon is a processed red meat rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which can amplify inflammatory pathways; chilli is a nightshade ingredient that may trigger sensitivity in some individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request reduced bacon or a turkey bacon substitute to lower arachidonic acid intake, and should omit chilli if nightshade sensitivity is present; olive oil and mushrooms provide anti-inflammatory MUFAs and beta-glucans that partially offset the pro-inflammatory components.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Alla Pastora is an Italian-inspired pasta dish served in Singapore's casual dining and food delivery market. The name translates loosely as 'shepherd-style' (from the Italian 'pastore', meaning shepherd), evoking a rustic, hearty preparation. The dish features cooked pasta — typically spaghetti or penne — tossed in a rich sautéed sauce of diced bacon and sliced mushrooms, finished with olive oil, garlic, and a hint of dried or fresh chilli. The flavour profile is savoury and umami-forward, with the smokiness of bacon balanced by the earthiness of mushrooms and the fruity warmth of olive oil. The texture is silky and lightly coated rather than heavily sauced. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 187.5 kcal, 7.5 g protein, 11.25 g fat (of which 4.5 g is saturated), and 15 g carbohydrate, with 275 mg sodium. A standard restaurant serving of 400 g delivers approximately 750 kcal, 60 g carbohydrate, and 1100 mg sodium. Clinically, the sodium and saturated fat content from bacon are the primary concerns for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The estimated glycaemic index of 45 (low) means glucose release is moderated by the fat and protein matrix, making moderate portions acceptable for Type 2 Diabetes management with appropriate portion control. The dish is not suitable for halal or vegetarian diets due to the bacon content. Known in Singapore as 牧羊人意大利面 (Chinese), Pasta Alla Pastora (Malay), அல்லா பாஸ்டோரா பாஸ்தா (Tamil), and अल्ला पास्टोरा पास्ता (Hindi).
common_user_queries: How many calories in Alla Pastora pasta?
Is Alla Pastora suitable for diabetics?
Is Alla Pastora high in sodium?
Can I eat Alla Pastora if I have high blood pressure?
What is the glycemic index of pasta alla pastora?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Alla Pastora exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy component data for pasta, bacon, mushrooms, and olive oil, and are consistent with the input record values. Confidence is medium because the dish composition is well-understood but the exact restaurant formulation is not independently verified by an approved source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this specific restaurant pasta preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo, or any other approved source as of 2024. The input record data_source is listed as 'grabfood', which is a forbidden source under Project Veritas v2 source policy; all nutrient values have been independently cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central component data and are retained only where consistent with approved-source estimates. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for white pasta (GI ~46); the fat and protein matrix is expected to modestly lower effective GI. This dish is not suitable for halal diets due to bacon content. The 'Pizza' ethnic tag in the original input has been removed as it is not applicable to this pasta dish. Country of origin is listed as Italy; the dish is served in Singapore via food delivery platforms. Multilingual local names added for Singapore market context: Chinese (牧羊人意大利面 / 牧羊人意大利麵), Malay (Pasta Alla Pastora), Tamil (அல்லா பாஸ்டோரா பாஸ்தா), Hindi (अल्ला पास्टोरा पास्ता).
